title: Epidendrum luteolum
taxonomy: Epidendrum luteolum is classified within the family Orchidaceae, commonly known as the orchid family. This family is one of the largest and most diverse groups of flowering plants. The genus Epidendrum is a genus of orchids, and it is native to tropical and subtropical regions of the Americas. The classification of Epidendrum luteolum is further divided into the subfamily Epidendroideae, which is a subfamily within the Orchidaceae family. This classification system helps to organize and understand the relationships between different species of orchids.

intro: Epidendrum luteolum, also known as the yellow epidendrum, is a type of orchid native to Central and South America, characterized by its bright yellow flowers and unique growth habit. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, offering potential health benefits including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial activities, which may aid in the treatment of various ailments such as respiratory issues and skin infections. The bioactive constituents of Epidendrum luteolum include alkaloids, flavonoids, and phenolic compounds, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, the plant is commonly used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are often employed to treat respiratory conditions, such as bronchitis and asthma.
